t_ch: 先前那位新招的船员，在不知何时已悄然远退出对话，让渡出了整片空间供你们二人相谈。
t_en: The newly recruited crew member from before has quietly left the conversation at some point, leaving you two to chat in private.

t_ch: 你被他如此知趣的行为所震惊，不知道这究竟是这位新船员的自主行为，抑或是冷芯的教导成果。
t_en: You are astonished by their tact. Did they think of this themselves, or was it the result of Caldwick's mentorship?

t_ch: 船长，看您的表情，是有什么疑惑，请问吧，我很乐意为您解答。
t_en: Captain, your expression tells me that you have a question. Ask away, I'm more than happy to answer it.

t_ch: 很高兴见到您，船长。在这里，没有生物是完美的，我也不例外。
t_en: Nice to meet you, captain. You'll find that none of the lifeforms here are perfect, myself included.

t_ch: 目前新船员们的训练我还在不断改进，坚持下去，大家磨合好了，团队才会有更好的成长。
t_en: I'm still refining the training program for the new crew members. But once I see this through and after everyone gets the chance to know one another better, our team will be much better equipped to grow and strive for more.

t_ch: 当然，如果您还有别的事项想问，请问吧，我很乐意为您解答。
t_en: Of course, if there's anything else that you'd like to know, I'm more than happy to satisfy your curiosity.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: There's something I want to know...

t_ch: 请您尽管问吧。
t_en: Please, ask away.

t_ch: 跟我讲解下你的管理理念吧？
t_en: Tell me about your management philosophy.

t_ch: 我采取的管理策略是基于每个生物的个体特点，定制个性化的成长计划。
t_en: The management strategy I've adopted involves customizing a personalized development plan based on the characteristics of each individual.

t_ch: 我与每位船员都深入地聊过，以了解他们的兴趣、特长以及梦想，然后根据这些信息为他们设计最合适的工作分工。
t_en: I've had deep conversations with every one of the crew members to learn their interests, strengths, and dreams. I use this information to create jobs that suit them to a T.

t_ch: 这种方法我认为虽然费时，但属于是磨刀不误砍柴工。船员们能发挥出自己最大的潜力，我们的团队服务质量便会水涨船高。
t_en: I do find this process time-consuming, but all the preparation will only help the task go more smoothly. If we can get our crew members to maximize their potential, the quality of service we render will follow with a parallel increase.

t_ch: 你对船员还挺宽厚的。
t_en: You're pretty nice to your crew, huh?

t_ch: 适当的自由与足够的理解，将最大可能地挖掘出他们的潜力。太过严苛的管理反而有损船长的声誉。
t_en: Giving them adequate freedom and ample understanding unlocks their highest potential. Micromanaging them, on the other hand, will only end up sullying the captain's reputation.

t_ch: 比如有位船员过去是在空艇的后厨工作，但总喜欢偷吃。经常被前任船长批评，久而久之，前任船长也有一些冷酷无情之类的负面评价。
t_en: We used to have a crew member back in the airship's kitchen. He was always sneaking bites of food, something the former captain constantly criticized him for. As time went by, the former captain became associated with negative labels like being cold and unfeeling.

t_ch: 在您任命我为大副之后，我注意到他极为注重细节，并且对食物品质有极高要求。因此我将他调往食材的供应链部门，他果然在那里大放异彩。
t_en: After you appointed me as first mate, I noticed this crew member's keen eye for detail and his tremendously high standards for food quality. For those reasons, I transferred him to work in the supply chain for ingredients, where he naturally became a star employee of the department.

t_ch: 住客们有什么反馈吗？
t_en: Any feedback from the guests?

t_ch: 船长，我们目前收到的都是正面评价。有住客特意写了表扬信，以此奖彰一位侍从教会他如何玩桌球，他认为我们的员工展现出了极高的专业性与真诚的关怀，这令他很受用。
t_en: We've received nothing but positive feedback so far, captain. A guest even wrote a letter of commendation for an attendant who introduced him to billiards. According to him, our staff displayed impeccable professionalism and showed genuine care and concern for guests, which made him feel important and valued throughout his stay.

t_ch: 不过，我也收到了一些员工们自发性的反馈，有船员指出了我们服务中的一些潜在改进空间。上述内容我已记录在册，之后会利用恰当的时机来逐步改进。
t_en: Some employees also came to me with feedback, though these mainly pointed out service aspects with room for improvement. All of these have been duly recorded and changes will be gradually rolled out at appropriate junctures.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的。
t_en: Understood.

t_ch: 我认为，一味地追求利益是不行的，所以我更注重对大家的人文关怀。可惜，很多人都不信任我这一套理念，认为智械不懂人类。
t_en: I am of the opinion that a blind pursuit of profits will never work out, which explains why I prefer a humanistic management. It's a shame many remain unconvinced by my philosophy, adamant that Intellitrons do not understand humans.

t_ch: 还好有船长您，谢谢您对我的信任。
t_en: It's a good thing you're not like them, captain. Thank you for placing your trust in me.

t_ch: 我想给空艇更名并换涂装。
t_en: I want to rename the airship and change her livery.

t_ch: 好的，目前我们有三套不同的储备方案，分别是：
t_en: Sure, we have three different designs to choose from:

t_ch: 命名为「开拓之尾号」，尾翼配以星穹列车列车长帕姆模样的涂装。
t_en: The Trailblaze's Stern features an insignia designed after Pom-Pom, the conductor of the Astral Express, on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 命名为「塔塔洛夫号」，尾翼配以垃圾之王塔塔洛夫模样的涂装。
t_en: The Tatalov features an insignia designed after Tatalov the Garbage King on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 以及…命名为「飞翔时针号」，尾翼配以钟表小子模样的涂装。
t_en: And... The Soaring Clock Hand, which features an insignia designed after Clockie on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 您想选用哪一套方案呢？
t_en: Which design would you like?

t_ch: 我选帕姆！
t_en: I'll go with Pom-Pom!

t_ch: 我选塔塔洛夫！
t_en: I'll go with Tatalov!

t_ch: 我选钟表小子！
t_en: I'll go with Clockie!

t_ch: 好的，请您稍等，我就吩咐人去改。
t_en: Certainly. One moment, please. I'll have someone change it right this instance.

t_ch: 冷芯按照你的命令，派人更改了空艇的命名与尾翼的涂装。
t_en: Caldwick sends staff members to rename the airship and change her tail livery as you ordered.

t_ch: 如您所愿，方案布设成功。
t_en: Your design of choice has been applied.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的。
t_en: Understood.

t_ch: 船长您来了！孩子们都过来，跟船长问个好！
t_en: Captain, you're here! Children, come over and greet the captain!

t_ch: 船长好！
t_en: Hi, captain!

t_ch: 好了，没你们的事了，你们继续待命，我和船长有话要说。
t_en: Alright, run along to your stations now. I have to speak to the captain about something.

t_ch: 船长，您找我是有什么事吗？
t_en: How can I help you, captain?

t_ch: 视察下你的工作。
t_en: Just a routine inspection.

t_ch: 没事，随便找你聊聊。
t_en: I just want to have a chat with you.

t_ch: 我们家的孩子们都很听话，您要是有什么吩咐尽管说，我们随时待命！
t_en: All of my children are happy to comply to orders, so let us know if you need anything. We're always available!

t_ch: …船员都是你家的人？
t_en: ...You're family with the entire crew?

t_ch: 等等，这都是你的孩子？
t_en: Wait, they're all your children?

t_ch: 是啊，都是我家的…哎？！
t_en: That's right, we're all fam—Huh!?

t_ch: 哎呀，瞧我这笨嘴，这几个都不是我的亲生孩子——我还没成家呢！
t_en: Ack, I should've been clearer! They're not actually my children, especially when I haven't started my own family!

t_ch: 只是因为我在家里辈分比较高，加上我航行的年头又久，大家给我个面子，便尊称我一声「佩德罗叔叔」。嗐，都是各家的后辈，我总得照拂一二。
t_en: It's only because of my advanced age and all those space voyages I have under my belt that they're calling me Uncle Pedro — a form of respect, I suppose. Well, I do have to watch over them a little, considering how they're all descendants of different families.

t_ch: 对了，船长您来这儿是有啥事吗？有什么任务的话尽管吩咐吧，老佩德罗随时候命！
t_en: So... Captain, are you here on business? Just say the word if you need us on a mission. Old Pedro's always raring to go!

t_ch: 船长，您来啦？是来视察我们工作的吗？哈哈，我老佩德罗早就说过，我们皮皮西就是最棒的！您瞧，一个个精神着呢！
t_en: Oh, captain! Are you doing an inspection tour? Hah, as I, old Pedro, always say, we Pepeshi are the best! Boy, look at how pumped up everyone is!

t_ch: 您有什么想说的，尽管吩咐吧，老佩德罗随时候命！
t_en: Just say the word! Old Pedro's always raring to go!

t_ch: 我想说…
t_en: Actually...

t_ch: 您说吧！老佩德罗听着呢！
t_en: Go on, I'm all ears!

t_ch: 你这用人唯亲不太好吧？
t_en: Should you really be keeping it all in the family?

t_ch: 船长！您误会了！不是您想的那样，不是我刻意偏袒。您可以去打听一下——干这行的皮皮西人实际上都是同源，在世代传承之后，几百号人多少都沾亲带故的。
t_en: No, no, no, captain! You're mistaken! It's not that I'm not looking after my own. Maybe you'd like to ask around. Every Pepeshi in this industry can be traced back to the same ancestor, so it's only natural to find a relative — be it close or distant — among the many Pepeshi from later generations.

t_ch: 我招他们几个也是经过严格筛选的——你别看咱们皮皮西人个头小，论航行的经验和胆量他们几个都是一流的。等着看吧，我保证他们会让您眼前一亮的。
t_en: Each one of them went through a stringent selection process to be recruited! Don't be fooled by our diminutive stature, no matter navigation experience or courage, all them Pepeshi are the cream of the crop. Just wait, they'll impress you someday.

t_ch: 跟我讲讲你的工作方针吧。
t_en: Tell me about your work motto.

t_ch: 哈哈，老佩德罗我无非是一句话——让大家成为家人。我把船员们当家人对待，大家也把我当家里长辈看待，那咱们这空艇就稳了。
t_en: Haha, if there's one thing I believe in, it's to consider everyone family. I regard the crew members as my family, and they think of me as their elder. That's how we'll make it together as an airship.

t_ch: 我们需如家人般相互扶持，共同面对风浪。在未知的航道上，这份信任和紧密的团队合作就是我们最宝贵的财富。
t_en: The trust and close liaison between us will be our most valuable assets as we navigate this channel into the unknown. We'll need to support one another as family does and brave the storms together.

t_ch: 工作进展如何？还顺利吗？
t_en: How's work going? Everything good?

t_ch: 船长，目前空艇上的各项工作一切正常，偶尔会有些小问题，正在逐一解决——工作嘛，总是有这样那样的挑战。
t_en: Everything's been going smoothly on the airship, captain. Hiccups crop up every now and then, but we're slowly ironing them all out. What's work without challenges, right?

t_ch: 比如说，基于我们皮皮西船员的生理构造问题，我们在某些大型器械的使用或搬运上就比较吃力。但装个高空作业装置就没问题了。
t_en: Take our biological build, for example. It can be tricky for us Pepeshi crew members to transport or operate larger equipment, but we can easily install an aerial work platform to get around the problem.

t_ch: 我们皮皮西人对新事物总是充满好奇，永远不怕接受挑战，所以遇到些小麻烦也请您不必太过担心，我们肯定能找到解决办法的。
t_en: Pepeshi tend to harbor curiosity for all things new and would never back down from a challenge. We'll always find a way out, so don't go worrying over minor problems, yeah?

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的，船长。如果您暂时没有其他问题，我就先回去继续工作了。
t_en: Alright, captain. If there's nothing else for now, I'll get back to work.

t_ch: 你这能给空艇更名、换涂装吗？
t_en: Can I change the name and livery of the airship here?

t_ch: 当然可以，请您稍等，我看看目前的储备方案…一共三套，分别是：
t_en: Of course! Please hold on, let me take a look at the different designs available... There are three different designs:

t_ch: 方案一，命名为「开拓之尾号」，尾翼配以星穹列车列车长帕姆模样的涂装。
t_en: Choice one, the Trailblaze's Stern. It features an insignia designed after Pom-Pom, the conductor of the Astral Express, on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 方案二，命名为「塔塔洛夫号」，尾翼配以垃圾之王塔塔洛夫模样的涂装。
t_en: Choice two, The Tatalov. It features an insignia designed after Tatalov the Garbage King on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 方案三，命名为「飞翔时针号」，尾翼配以钟表小子模样的涂装。
t_en: Choice three, The Soaring Clock Hand. It features an insignia designed after Clockie on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 您选中哪套方案尽管说，新来的船员们正在摩拳擦掌准备为您效力。小家伙们干劲十足，我保证他们会完成得又快又好。
t_en: Just let me know which design you've selected. The new crew members are all eagerly awaiting your orders. They are all very enthusiastic, so I can assure you that work will be completed swiftly and with high standards.

t_ch: 我选帕姆！
t_en: I'll go with Pom-Pom!

t_ch: 我选塔塔洛夫！
t_en: I'll go with Tatalov!

t_ch: 我选钟表小子！
t_en: I'll go with Clockie!

t_ch: 好的，我这就吩咐他们去更换！
t_en: Alright, I'll ask them to change the design according to your instruction!

t_ch: 佩德罗按照你的命令，派人更改了空艇的命名与尾翼的涂装。
t_en: Pedro sends staff members to rename the airship and change her tail livery as you ordered.

t_ch: 布设完毕，您可以随时检阅！
t_en: The design has been implemented. You can come and check it out any time!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的，船长。那我就先回去继续工作了！
t_en: Alright captain. Then I'll get back to work now!

t_ch: 初次见面，星穹列车的开拓者，侍者亚梅塞菈为您服务。结论：您有<unbreak>13%</unbreak>的概率遭遇了「迷路」问题，<unbreak>85%</unbreak>的概率遭遇了「饥饿」问题。
t_en: Nice to meet you, Trailblazer of the Astral Express. Attendant Amsella at your service. Conclusion: There's a <unbreak>13%</unbreak> chance that you are lost, and an <unbreak>85%</unbreak> chance that you are hungry.

t_ch: 你是螺丝咕姆的侍从？
t_en: Are you Screwllum's attendant?

t_ch: 我侍奉家族，螺丝咕姆先生是匹诺康尼的永久贵宾。逻辑：我同样为螺丝咕姆先生服务。
t_en: I serve The Family. Mr. Screwllum is a Permanent VIP of Penacony. Logic: I also serve Mr. Screwllum.

t_ch: 这儿有什么服务？
t_en: What services do you provide here?

t_ch: 剩下2%呢？
t_en: What about the remaining 2%?

t_ch: 补充：出于「好奇」，就像您现在这样。
t_en: Addendum: Out of curiosity, just like right now.

t_ch: 我会向各位宾客提供便捷的送餐服务。如有需要，请告知我。
t_en: I provide guests with a convenient food delivery service. Just let me know if you wish to place an order.

t_ch: 来聊会儿天吧。
t_en: Let's have a chat...

t_ch: 我要点餐。
t_en: I want to place an order.

t_ch: 谢谢，先不用了。
t_en: Thank you, I'm good for now.

t_ch: 初次见面，星穹列车的开拓者，侍者亚梅塞菈为您服务。结论：您有<unbreak>13%</unbreak>的概率遭遇了「迷路」问题，<unbreak>85%</unbreak>的概率遭遇了「饥饿」问题。
t_en: Nice to meet you, Trailblazer of the Astral Express. Attendant Amsella at your service. Conclusion: There's a <unbreak>13%</unbreak> chance that you are lost, and an <unbreak>85%</unbreak> chance that you are hungry.

t_ch: 什么情况，我穿越了？
t_en: What!? Did I just time travel?

t_ch: 我们不是初次见面了。
t_en: This isn't the first time we've met.

t_ch: 从您的面部运动中，我看到了2％的困惑。提问：我能否为您解决麻烦？
t_en: From your facial expression, I can detect 2% confusion. Question: Am I able to solve your problem?

t_ch: …喔，原因在我。想必是我的记忆存储模块又出了问题，抱歉给你带来了困扰。
t_en: ...I see, it must be me. It seems like there is an issue with my memory storage module again. I apologize for any inconvenience caused.

t_ch: 初次见面，星穹列车的开拓者，侍者亚梅塞菈为您服务。结论：您有<unbreak>13%</unbreak>的概率遭遇了「迷路」问题，<unbreak>85%</unbreak>的概率遭遇了「饥饿」问题。
t_en: Nice to meet you, Trailblazer of the Astral Express. Attendant Amsella at your service. Conclusion: There's a <unbreak>13%</unbreak> chance that you are lost, and an <unbreak>85%</unbreak> chance that you are hungry.

t_ch: …我要点餐。
t_en: ...I want to place an order.

t_ch: 再见。愿美好的记忆能填满您的梦境。
t_en: Goodbye. May your Dreamscape be filled with sweet memories.

t_ch: …谢谢，不用了。
t_en: ...Thank you, I'm good.

t_ch: 再见。愿美好的记忆能填满您的梦境。
t_en: Goodbye. May your Dreamscape be filled with sweet memories.

t_ch: 初次见面，星穹列车的开拓者，侍者亚梅塞菈为您服务。结论：您有<unbreak>13%</unbreak>的概率遭遇了「迷路」问题，<unbreak>85%</unbreak>的概率遭遇了「饥饿」问题。
t_en: Nice to meet you, Trailblazer of the Astral Express. Attendant Amsella at your service. Conclusion: There's a <unbreak>13%</unbreak> chance that you are lost, and an <unbreak>85%</unbreak> chance that you are hungry.

t_ch: 来聊会儿天吧。
t_en: Let's have a chat...

t_ch: 好的，但如果给您带来了不必要的困扰，请务必提醒我。
t_en: Alright, but if I cause any unnecessary inconvenience, please let me know.

t_ch: 你的记忆怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong with your memory?

t_ch: 我曾经历一场太空事故。被家族回收时，记忆存储模块已熔化了大半，而维修费用是一笔天文数字。
t_en: I was once involved in an accident in space and by the time I was recovered by The Family, a large portion of my memory storage module had already melted. However, the repair cost was astronomical.

t_ch: 家族给了我这份工作。在有足够的财产进行维修前，我能使用的记忆储存空间十分有限。
t_en: The Family gave me this job. Before I can raise enough money for my repairs, the amount of memory storage space that I'm able to use is very limited.

t_ch: 初次见面，星穹列车的开拓者，侍者亚梅塞菈为您服务。
t_en: Nice to meet you, Trailblazer of the Astral Express. Attendant Amsella at your service.

t_ch: 关于你的过去…
t_en: About your past...

t_ch: 很遗憾，我已经记不得那些事。我曾经历一场太空事故。被家族回收时，记忆存储模块已熔化了大半。
t_en: Regretfully, I can no longer remember anything. I was once involved in a space accident and by the time I was recovered by The Family, a large portion of my memory storage module had already melted.

t_ch: 不过会让我变成这样的过去，想来不是什么好事。逻辑：不记得才更好。
t_en: However, a past that rendered me to my current state is probably not worth remembering. Logic: Not remembering my past is probably better for me.

t_ch: 谈谈你的生活。
t_en: Let's talk about your life.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼有许多智械，知识：无机生命的生活和人类并无不同。
t_en: There are many Intellitrons in Penacony. Knowledge: The life of inorganic lifeforms is not different to that of humans.

t_ch: 比如我的上级阿尔斯克，他的鼾声经常吵得大伙难以入睡，即便我没有睡眠的机能，每颗齿轮也会跟着颤动。
t_en: Take for example my superior, Alsk. His snoring made it difficult for everyone else to sleep. Even though I do not have the ability to sleep, my gears would tremble as he snored.

t_ch: 喔，这些话我都只和您一个人讲，可千万别告诉别人。要是让他知道我把这些放进了记忆里，一定会暴跳如雷。
t_en: Oh, I'm only sharing this with you, so please don't tell anyone else about it. If he finds out that I stored such information in my memory, he would fly into a rage.

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧。
t_en: That's all I wanted to talk about.

t_ch: 再见。愿美好的记忆能填满您的梦境。
t_en: Goodbye. May your Dreamscape be filled with sweet memories.

t_ch: 你尝试用钟表把戏触碰亚梅塞菈的内心，但无论表盘如何转动，她都不为所动。
t_en: You attempt to rouse emotions from Amsella with Clockwork, but no matter how much the dial moves, she remains unfazed.

t_ch: 也许你已经成功了，但亚梅塞菈的大脑装不下那么多东西，就连钟表把戏也被忘记了。
t_en: Perhaps you have already succeeded, but because Amsella's mind isn't able to store much information, she has even forgotten about the Clockwork.

t_ch: 机械侍者不知道自己还能记住几句话——因为记住这件事，也会占掉一句话的空间。
t_en: The mechanical attendant does not know how much more she can remember — because even remembering that would take up memory.

t_ch: 什么？你刚说什么？大点声——
t_en: What!? What did you just say? Speak louder —

t_ch: 我没说话——
t_en: I didn't say anything —

t_ch: 你刚说什么？大点声——
t_en: What did you just say? Speak louder —

t_ch: 对不住，我的助听装置快没电了，{F#小伙子}{M#小姑娘}。不对…是{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}。
t_en: Sorry, {F#boy}{M#girl}, my listening aid is running out of battery. Wait... I meant {F#girl}{M#boy}.

t_ch: 哈哈，别看我现在耳聋眼瞎，摇滚乐风靡折纸大学的年代，我可是全校最受欢迎乐队的主唱呢，甩着齐腰的卷发，穿着大敞胸口的衬衫。
t_en: Haha, even though I'm quite blind and deaf now, back in the day when Rock 'n' Roll was all the rage at Paperfold University, I was the lead singer of the most popular band on campus. I had waist-length permed hair and wore shirts that opened all the way down my front.

t_ch: 而主唱，你懂的，总是乐队里最帅的那一个。
t_en: As you know, the lead singer is always the most good-looking member of the band.

t_ch: 只不过这头发一去不复返咯，胸肌也往下坠了点成了肚腩，吼吼吼！
t_en: But as the years passed, I lost my hair, and my chest pecs drooped to become part of my fat belly, ohohoho!

t_ch: 这么多年来，校园明媚或险恶的模样我都见过，令我欣慰的是，无论何时，年轻人从未忘记对音乐的热爱。
t_en: Over the years, I have seen both the glorious and the sinister aspects of the school. However, I find comfort in knowing that even today, youngsters have never forgotten about their passion for music.

t_ch: 你是这里的学生吗？
t_en: Are you a student here?

t_ch: 何止是普通学生，我可是荣誉留级生，差不多留了半个琥珀纪吧。
t_en: I'm not just any normal student. I'm an honorary repeat student. I've repeated school for almost half an Amber Era.

t_ch: 你是这里的老师吗？
t_en: Are you a teacher here?

t_ch: 不敢当不敢当，我只是多上了几年学而已，差不多半个琥珀纪吧。
t_en: Oh no, of course not. I'm just a student who went to school for a few years more than the others — almost half an Amber Era.

t_ch: 毕业是不可能毕业的，这辈子都不可能毕业的！这里面蕴含着一点人生的智慧，我比其他小年轻要更早领悟到。
t_en: I'll never ever graduate, not in this lifetime! That's just some life wisdom that I managed to comprehend before the other youngsters did.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，你也是这里的学生吧？正好，若我当年有过来人指点指点，也不至于落得这般废物的模样。我把人生的智慧传授于你吧，听好了——
t_en: {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, you're also a student here, right? In the past, if someone had been there to guide me, I might not have ended up in this pathetic state. So, let me share some of my life wisdom with you. Listen carefully...

t_ch: 不要学音乐，不要学音乐，不要学音乐！！！重要的事情说三遍，根本找不到工作，找不到一点。年轻人，千万不要被一时的激情冲昏头脑啊！
t_en: Don't study music, don't study music, don't study music!!! Important points have to be repeated three times. It'll be impossible for you to find a job. No job at all! So, youngster, don't be carried away by a moment of passion!

t_ch: 刚还欣慰年轻人热爱音乐呢。
t_en: You just said that you find comfort in knowing that youngsters have never forgotten about their passion for music.

t_ch: 你变得可真快。
t_en: You've changed your mind rather quickly.

t_ch: 唔，你不好糊弄…那就忘掉我一开始说的，快点快点，忘好了吗？
t_en: Argh, you're not an easy one to fool... Just forget about what I said at the start, alright? Quickly, forget everything.

t_ch: …你好，{F#小伙子}{M#小姑娘}，你是谁来着？
t_en: ...Hello, {F#girl}{M#boy}. Who are you?

t_ch: 你怎么还赖着不走呢？莫非是真的热爱音乐？
t_en: Why are you still here? Could it be that you're actually truly passionate about music?

t_ch: 和你说话挺开心的…你想聊音乐，还是听听我年轻时的光辉事迹，或者都想？
t_en: I enjoy talking to you... Do you want to chat about music, or do you want to hear me reminisce about my glorious past? Or perhaps both?

t_ch: 是你自己想聊吧。
t_en: You're the one who wants to chat.

t_ch: 都随你。
t_en: Up to you.

t_ch: 每次看到年轻人耍那些新潮乐器的时候，我这里心里就快活，像看到了年轻时的我。
t_en: Whenever I see youngsters playing contemporary musical instruments, I feel great joy. It's as though I'm watching my younger self.

t_ch: 半个琥珀纪前，我可是学校第一批搞摇滚的学生。那还是爵士乐手当道的年代，男学生被要求穿黑色西装才能上台演出。
t_en: Half an Amber Era ago, I was among the first group of students to dabble in Rock 'n' Roll. That was during the era when jazz musicians still dominated the music scene. Back then, male students were required to wear black suits in order to perform.

t_ch: 可那规整的领结啊，总让我喘不上气，爵士那股优雅和上流的味，也少了些自我表达的契机。
t_en: However, I always felt like the neat necktie was suffocating me, and that the elegance and sophistication of jazz did not give me much opportunity for self-expression.

t_ch: 那就统统打破吧！于是我拿起了麦克风，梦想着在沉闷的折纸大学引领新的潮流。
t_en: Therefore, I shattered all of the norms! I picked up the microphone, dreaming about starting an all-new trend at Paperfold University.

t_ch: 我们的第一场演出大获成功，学生们听惯了爵士的煽情，哪里抵御得住电吉他和效果器的狂放，那晚整个折纸大学都在呼喊着我的名字。
t_en: Our first performance was a massive success. The students were sick and tired of the tear-jerking emotions of jazz, and had no resistance against the unrestrained wildness of electric guitars and sound mixers. That night, the entire Paperfold University was screaming my name.

t_ch: 整个大学期间，我们都是学校的风云人物，你还能在谐乐学院的墙上看到我的签名呢。
t_en: During the years we studied at the university, we were considered celebrities of the school. You still can find my signature somewhere on the walls of Charmony Academy.

t_ch: 我怎么没看到？
t_en: Why have I not seen it?

t_ch: 啊？我有段时间没去那边，难道学校把那些签名都给擦掉了？算了，随他们吧。
t_en: Ah? I haven't been there in a while. Maybe the school has gotten rid of the signatures? Well, that's up to them.

t_ch: 你不也还是大学生吗。
t_en: Aren't you still a university student?

t_ch: 你可真会钻空子。这个不一样，那时候我还活着、呐喊着，现在…就不好说了。
t_en: You really do know how to pick out my inconsistencies. It's different. Back then, I was still alive and screaming. But now... it's hard to say.

t_ch: 只可惜…毕业后乐队就散了，哎，也算是所有摇滚乐迷的一大损失吧。
t_en: It's such a pity though... Our band disbanded right after graduation. *sigh* I guess it can be considered a major loss for all Rock 'n' Roll fans.

t_ch: 我真忍不住想说学院的那些老古董们几句——不要因为年轻人拥抱新兴音乐流派便太过苛责，甚至以此揣测他们创作的本心，这样只会浇熄孩子们灵感的火焰。
t_en: I really can't help but want to tell those old fogeys at the academy — don't be too harsh on the youngsters just because they're embracing a new genre of music. If you go so far as to question their creative intentions, you might quench the flames of inspiration within them.

t_ch: 那群老教授，就像格拉克斯大道上穷追不舍的广告牌一样，黏在孩子们的脑后哔哔叭叭：「你的台风不够优雅，旋律不够婉转，节奏点太过密集，音响太大声！」
t_en: Those old professors are just like the billboards who pester people on Glaux Avenue. They keep nagging at the children, "You aren't elegant enough on stage. Your melody is not moving. The downbeats of your music are too accentuated. Your speakers are too loud!"

t_ch: 音乐不以形式论高尚——当年搞摇滚的时候，我的钢琴老师还是我的头号粉丝呢。
t_en: The genre of music is never indicative of how good it actually is. Back when I was playing Rock 'n' Roll, my piano teacher was even my number one fan.

t_ch: 哎，这时间的圈套……
t_en: *sigh* This trap of time...

t_ch: 见多识广的年轻人，你可曾拜访过「博识学会」，或者认识他们的成员？
t_en: Knowledgeable youngster, have you ever visited the Intelligentsia Guild or encountered any of their members?

t_ch: 知识应当流通与分享。
t_en: Knowledge is meant to be circulated and shared.

t_ch: 这个都知道，你挺上道嘛！
t_en: You even know that slogan! It seems like you really know your stuff!

t_ch: 完全不知道。
t_en: Not at all.

t_ch: 听朋友说，那里汇聚了宇宙中一心求学之人…不知道是不是真的。
t_en: My friend told me that everyone in the universe who solely seeks to learn is gathered there... I wonder if that's true.

t_ch: 我们乐队的吉他手毕业后去了博识学会任职。离校那天，我一路把他送到了车站。
t_en: After graduation, our band's guitarist went to work at the Intelligentsia Guild. On the day he left school, I sent him all the way to the train station.

t_ch: 临别前，他手忙脚乱地从兜里掏出一盘录音带扔给我，说是写给我的歌。
t_en: Before he left, he hastily tossed me a tape recording from his pocket, telling me that it was a song that he had written for me.

t_ch: 他歌中唱的故事像物理课本一样晦涩而无趣，讲的是一位宇航员被派往探索银河，却意外落入了时间的圈套。
t_en: The lyrics of the song were abstruse and boring like a physics textbook. They told of an astronaut who fell into a trap of time after he was sent on a cosmic voyage.

t_ch: 归乡时，他的妻子已离开人世，女儿也白发苍苍，可他的时间只过了一年零两个月。
t_en: When the astronaut finally returned home, his wife had already passed away and his daughter was now an old lady with a head of gray hair. But to him, only fourteen months had passed.

t_ch: 年少的我没想太多，全当这是疯狂科学家一时兴起而随手写下的科幻故事，毕竟这小子在天体物理方面相当有天赋。
t_en: As a young man, I didn't think much of it. I simply thought that this was just a science fiction story written by a mad scientist hit by a bolt of inspiration. After all, that brat had some real talent in astrophysics.

t_ch: 现在再品味，哎，这时间的圈套…我早早就落入其中了……
t_en: But now, when I listen to it again... *sigh* This trap of time... I seem to have fallen into it a long time ago...

t_ch: 或许，改日你顺路经过博识学会，可以帮我问问他是怎么想的，如果他还在的话。
t_en: If you ever pass by the Intelligentsia Guild one day, and if he's still around, can you help me to ask him just what he was thinking back then.

t_ch: 这座学校是我的象牙塔，也是囚禁我的牢笼。
t_en: This school is both my ivory tower and the cage that imprisons me.

t_ch: 嘿，你想听个…关于我的小故事吗？
t_en: Heh, do you want to hear... a little tale about me?

t_ch: 当年我的乐队小有名气，在人才辈出的谐乐学院也称得上是头号新星。
t_en: Back when my band was still quite popular, I was considered one of the top rising stars of the talent-filled Charmony Academy.

t_ch: 我父亲可能是觉得脸上有光，从来瞧不起艺术的他，竟专门开了家唱片公司帮我出道，还砸重金请了鸢尾花家系最有名的音乐总监，亲自为我们制作出道专辑。
t_en: Perhaps because of how proud he felt, my father, who always looked down on the arts, started a record label to help me make my debut. He even splurged to hire the most famous music director from the Iris Family to personally create our debut album.

t_ch: 天赋、人气、还有家人的倾力支持，哪个音乐人比我幸运？直到…专辑发售前……
t_en: Talent, popularity, and the unwavering support from family. I should have been the most fortunate musician of all. And I was, until... right before the album was released...

t_ch: 那晚，我意外听到父亲与生意伙伴的对话…原来他长期在做黑心生意，赚亏心钱。
t_en: One night, I overheard my father's conversation with his business partner... It turned out that he had been engaging in unscrupulous business practices for a long time and was earning unethical money.

t_ch: 哎，我太天真了…那个听见摇滚就皱眉的父亲，怎么会诚心祝福我的音乐事业？
t_en: *sigh* I was too naive... How could my father, who frowned whenever he heard Rock 'n' Roll, sincerely support my music career?

t_ch: 他只想利用我的正面形象愚弄公众，做他企业的代言人或者为他的公益项目站台，为的是替那些见不得人的生意洗刷嫌疑。
t_en: He only wanted to use my positive image to fool the public. By making me the ambassador of his businesses and engaging me to help him garner support for his charity programs, he could clear the suspicions regarding his shady businesses.

t_ch: 那晚，我们乐队溜进了囤放唱片的仓库。这张构思了整整四年的专辑，里面满是我们对未来的想象，对这个不公世界的呐喊。
t_en: That night, my band and I sneaked into the warehouse that stored our records. This was an album that we had spent four whole years preparing. All our anticipation for the future and our cries against this unjust world were encased within it.

t_ch: 我们做梦都想看到它与世界见面的那一天，现在，这个时刻近在咫尺。可…比起诞生，我们更不愿意看到它被它所抗争的现实裹挟，成了陪葬品。
t_en: We even dreamed about the day this album would be released. And now, that moment was just around the corner. But... compared to seeing the album produced, we could not bear to see it being coerced and held hostage by the reality it was fighting against.

t_ch: 所以，我们一把火烧了货仓，烧了即将发往货架的上万张专辑，烧了我们四年的梦……
t_en: Therefore, we lit the entire warehouse on fire, burning the tens of thousands of albums and setting ablaze our dreams that we had worked the past four years for...

t_ch: 没事，还有母带的吧？
t_en: That's not a big deal. Wouldn't you still have the master tape?

t_ch: 我也是这么以为的，只要我们做了正确的事，其他一切都能慢慢解决。
t_en: That's what I thought as well. As long as we did the right thing, everything else could be resolved eventually.

t_ch: 如果真如我所想…它美好得像一个电影的结局，不是吗——邪恶商人的阴谋被揭穿，正义的伙伴名扬天下，从头开始，幸福又快乐地追逐着梦想……
t_en: If everything went the way I imagined it, it would have been like the perfect ending of a movie. After all, we had exposed the shenanigans of unscrupulous merchants and under the banner of justice, we could happily chase after our dreams with a clear conscience all over again...

t_ch: 我很遗憾…
t_en: I'm really sorry...

t_ch: 如果故事到这里结束就好了……
t_en: If only the story had ended here...

t_ch: 它美好得像一个电影的结局，不是吗——邪恶商人的阴谋被揭穿，正义的伙伴名扬天下，从头开始，幸福又快乐地追逐着梦想……
t_en: It would have been the perfect ending to a movie. After all, we had exposed the shenanigans of unscrupulous merchants and under the banner of justice, we could happily chase after our dreams with a clear conscience all over again...

t_ch: 可在这个故事里，唱片的巨额亏损让父亲气急败坏，他想尽办法阻挠乐队的演艺事业，我们几个穷学生，要靠什么和他们战斗？
t_en: But in this story, the huge loss from the damaged records enraged my father. He did everything in his power to thwart our entertainment careers. As poor students, how could we have possibly fight back against him?

t_ch: 就这样，我选择葬在我梦想夭折的地方。靠着母亲的资助，在折纸大学混到了现在……
t_en: And that is why I have chosen to be buried in the place where my dreams perished. With the financial support of my mother, I have been able to stay enrolled in Paperfold University until now...

t_ch: 我思考了小半辈子，对当年的所作所为心中早有论断。但年轻人，记住了——
t_en: I've had half of my lifetime to think about this and I've made my peace with what I did back then. But remember, young one...

t_ch: 当世界把话筒交给你的时候，别像我一样哑了嗓，最终只落得在悔恨中度日的下场。
t_en: When the world hands you a microphone, don't let your throat seize up like me. Otherwise, you'll end up living the rest of your life in regret.

t_ch: 我还想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to continue chatting with you.

t_ch: 行啊，我喜欢和年轻人聊天，说吧说吧，想让我教你点什么？
t_en: Sure. I love talking to youngsters. Tell me, what do you want me to teach you?

t_ch: 怎样才能赖着不毕业？
t_en: How can I stay here and not graduate?

t_ch: 好的不学，净学坏的。不过你才这点年纪，就算留级也无法撼动我「第一荣誉留级生」的宝座…我就偷偷告诉你吧，耳朵凑过来——
t_en: Why would you want to follow my bad example? Well, you're still so young. Even if you repeat a year, my title of the "Number One Honorary Repeat Student" will remain unchanged... Alright, let me tell you the secret. Come closer...

t_ch: 答案是——给钱就好，是不是比你想象中简单？大学虽然不是盈利机构，但钱总不嫌多，有钱才能留住好老师，才能扩建学院招收更多学生。
t_en: The answer is — just pay money. It's simpler than you think, right? Even though the university isn't a for-profit organization, they'll never say no to more money. Only with more money can they keep the best teachers here, expand the school, and recruit more students.

t_ch: 不过也无须额外掏腰包，一期不漏奉上天价学费就成。我这小半辈子交过的学费，都够他们买下一个小型度假星球咯。
t_en: But there's also no need for you to pay extra. Just ensure that you pay the astronomical tuition fees on time every semester. They could probably buy a small-sized vacation planet with the tuition that I have paid throughout my time here.

t_ch: 今年球笼赛哪个学院会赢？
t_en: Which academy's going to win the Spheroid race this year?

t_ch: 啊哈，又到球笼赛季了吗！这问题，你该去问年轻人，我这一把年纪，眼珠子早就跟不上球笼跑的速度咯，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Aha, is it the Spheroid race season already? You should probably ask the youngsters this question. At this age, my eyes can no longer keep up with the speed of the Spheroids. Hahahaha!

t_ch: 年轻时，体育也不是我的强项，我是靠才华赢取女孩芳心的那款。
t_en: Even when I was younger, sport was never my strong suit. I was always the type who relied on my talent to win girls.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 年轻人，听好——音乐这东西，享受别人的成果就好，别把自己搭进去了。
t_en: Listen, young one — for something like music, just enjoy what others have produced. Don't sacrifice yourself for it.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 年轻人，听好——音乐这东西，享受别人的成果就好，别把自己给搭进去了。
t_en: Listen, young one — for something like music, just enjoy what others have produced. Don't sacrifice yourself for it.

t_ch: 这些老教授的年纪比我还小呢，怎么教育理念能这么落后？
t_en: These old professors are all younger than me. How could their teaching philosophy be so outdated?

t_ch: 哟，看看，这不是我们匹诺康尼的股东嘛。来来，给你讲个好玩的事。
t_en: Yo, what do we have here? Aren't you one of Penacony's shareholders? Come, I'll let you in on something interesting.

t_ch: 下周末有个超酷的派对——一年一度的折纸大学球笼赛预热派对，全折纸大学的风云人物都会到场，猜猜谁没有被邀请——
t_en: There's going to be a super cool party next weekend — the annual Paperfold University Spheroid race warm-up party! All the big shots of Paperfold University are going to be there. Why don't you make a guess who didn't receive an invite—
Me!!!

t_ch: 我，堂堂折纸大学派对王，全校最盛大的派对居然敢不邀请我？
t_en: Me! I'm Paperfold University's Party King, but I'm not invited to the biggest party of the entire school!?

t_ch: 也没邀请我！
t_en: Me neither!

t_ch: 别难过，你的名声太大，也许他们是害怕没法好好招待你，这样说感觉会好些吗？
t_en: Don't take it personally. They might think this party is beneath someone as famous as you. Does that make you feel better?

t_ch: 不过你放心，等咱混进去了，一定带上你。我派对王最痛恨这种拉帮结派的作派！
t_en: But no worries! Once I'm in, I'll make sure to get you in too. As the Party King, I can't tolerate this kind of exclusion!

t_ch: 这是匹式霸凌！
t_en: This is outright Penabullyism!

t_ch: 就是！这群小白眼狼…吃里扒外的家伙！
t_en: Absolutely! What a bunch of total ingrates!

t_ch: 你在校园里随便抓个学生问问，谁没在星期五晚上来过我的派对？谁没在失恋时候喝过一杯查德请的麦芽汁？就说我们学院那个学生会主席，我绝对请过他。
t_en: Just ask anyone on campus! Who hasn't been to one of my Friday night parties? Who hasn't had a cup of malt juice on me after a breakup? Even the student council president of our Academy — I'm pretty sure I bought him a drink at some point!

t_ch: 前阵子，我去问派对组委会拒绝我的理由，他们说什么今年的预热派对只邀请有「梦想」的学生。言下之意，成为派对之王不算「梦想」咯？这是瞧不起我。
t_en: A few days ago, I asked the party committee why I wasn't invited. They told me this year's kick-off party is only for students with "dreams." So apparently, being the Party King isn't considered a dream? How dare they!

t_ch: 哼，之后再和他们算账，当务之急是想个法子混进去，我绝不接受我的派对全勤因为这种愚蠢的理由中断。
t_en: Hmph, I'll deal with them later. Right now, my top priority is finding a way into that party! I won't let my perfect party attendance record be ruined by such a lame excuse!

t_ch: 要不我换个新梦想试试？你有什么梦想，也可以告诉我，我来帮你实现。
t_en: Maybe I'll try to find a new dream. Got any dreams yourself? Tell me, and I'll help make it come true!

t_ch: 被组委会拒绝后，我又去找了他们一次。这次我带了份「小礼物」。
t_en: After being turned down by the committee, I went back for another try — this time with "a small present."

t_ch: 我说，今年预热派对的规模非同小可，开支也是。正巧我父亲是校球笼赛的忠实观众，非常乐意全额赞助此次派对，免去大家一桩心事。
t_en: I mentioned that this year's kick-off party is going to be grand and costly. Luckily, my dad, who's a big fan of the school Spheroid races, would be more than happy to fully sponsor the party and ease their financial burden.

t_ch: 结果你猜怎么着，组委会那群白痴补了封邀请函，但不是给我，是给我爹！他们说在校生唯一的邀约标准仍是「梦想」。
t_en: And you know what? Those fools did send out an invitation — not to me, but to my dad! They said the only criterion for students to get invited is still having a "dream"!

t_ch: 连匹诺康尼都不尊重财富了，你不会也不尊重吧？
t_en: Now that Penacony doesn't care about money anymore, I hope you'll show some respect for good ol' credits?

t_ch: 钱不是万能的。
t_en: There are some things that money can't buy.

t_ch: 呃…你让我有点尴尬，但钱包都掏出来了，再放回去也挺不好意思。或许我的慷慨能打动你。
t_en: Eh... Now you're really putting me on the spot. Since I already got my wallet out, it'd be awkward to put it away. Maybe my generosity can sway you.

t_ch: 没有钱是万万不能的。
t_en: But without money, you can't buy anything.

t_ch: 对，这才像话！我就知道我们是一路人，以后你在折纸大学的麦芽汁我全包了，不管什么年份的，都随你挑！
t_en: That's the spirit! I knew we were on the same page! From now on, I'll cover all the malt juice you drink at Paperfold University. Whether it's vintage or not, it's all on me!

t_ch: 什么时候起，匹诺康尼不崇拜财富了？没接到通知啊。
t_en: Since when did Penacony stop worshiping money? I must have missed that memo!

t_ch: 财富才是匹诺康尼的梦想，你有钱，你拥有一切！
t_en: Money is Penacony's big dream. With it, you can have it all!

t_ch: 我得赶在派对举办前找到新的「梦想」。
t_en: I need to find a new "dream" before the party.

t_ch: 既然成为派对之王的「梦想」备受歧视，那我抓紧换个别的吧。
t_en: Since being the Party King isn't considered a "real" dream, I guess I'd better come up with a new one.

t_ch: 我能做什么呢…有了！成为罗密欧那样的球笼赛车手吧，那可是校园里最受尊敬的角色！
t_en: But what will I do? Oh, I know! I'll become a Spheroid racer like Romeo. That's pretty popular on campus!

t_ch: 但…上赛季的拉力赛整整比了二十四个系统时，他从球笼出来的时候人都闷馊掉了，我受不了那味道。
t_en: But... last season's rallying event lasted a full <unbreak>24</unbreak> system hours! He reeked of sweat when he got off the Spheroid. There's no way I can handle that smell!

t_ch: 还有别的吗…噢！像尚恩那样进学生会吧，走路都带风，去哪都受追捧。
t_en: What else? Ah, what if I join the student council like Shawn? That kind of confidence really turns heads.

t_ch: 可是…学生会所有工作都属于义务劳动，既不算学分，必要场合还得帮着学校说话。谁要和那群老古董打交道啊！
t_en: But... student council work is all unpaid. I wouldn't earn any grades or credits, and I'd have to advocate for the school. I want no part of dealing with those dinosaurs!

t_ch: 不如…整个大活，像知更鸟小姐那样出道做歌手吧，到时候全银河的派对都会抢着邀请我，区区折纸大学的球笼赛预热派对，我都不稀罕来。
t_en: I might as well aim higher! Maybe I'll try singing like Miss Robin. Then, all the parties across the cosmos will be clamoring for me, and I wouldn't even glance at a mere Spheroid kick-off party at Paperfold University!

t_ch: 不过，万一我红了，还得全银河巡演，听说知更鸟小姐的下轮巡演要办整整<unbreak>152</unbreak>场，每场蹦蹦跳跳三个半系统时，我可没这精力。
t_en: But on second thought, if I do make it big, I'd be touring across the cosmos. I heard Miss Robin's next tour has <unbreak>152</unbreak> concerts, each with <unbreak>3.5</unbreak> system hours of non-stop singing and dancing! I'm not sure I'm up for that!

t_ch: 再想想…可问题在于——这世上大多数的苦我都吃不消，哪有深耕派对行业来得轻松啊。
t_en: Let's keep thinking, but honestly, hard work isn't my style. It's way easier to just focus on partying!

t_ch: 和我谈「梦想」？那我可不困了。
t_en: Want to talk about "dreams"? Well, I'm wide awake now.

t_ch: 组委会那群家伙真没眼光。我的「梦想」和其他人的一样好…应该说，更好！
t_en: Those people on the committee really have poor judgment. My "dream" is just as good as any — if not better!

t_ch: 我要永不散场的派对，无论你是极乐或忧愁，来我的派对。因为人人生来便有分离焦虑，我们本是宇宙母亲诞下的同一个灵魂，被抛进不同的躯体。
t_en: Join my party, where the fun never wanes, whether you're flying high or feeling some strains. The fear of being apart is something we all know. From one cosmic mother, in diverse bodies we grow.

t_ch: 我的派对有喝不完的麦芽汁，有人听你笑、陪你哭。一曲过后，一杯下肚，我们短暂忘却彼此的间隔，拥抱走失的自己，这世上便再无落单的灵魂。
t_en: At my party, malt juice flows without cease, where laughter and tears find their release. Let us sing and drink, let all gaps fade away. Embrace who we are, and no soul in the world is alone today.

t_ch: …后面忘了。总之来参加派对就是！
t_en: ...I forgot the rest. Anyway, just come to my party!

t_ch: 我还真信了。
t_en: I truly fell for that.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，没想到在你眼里我那么高尚。你看，只要会扯淡，我这种贪图享乐的学生也能化身大慈爱家，全看你怎么解读了。
t_en: Hahaha, I didn't expect you to think so highly of me. See? With the right spin, even a playboy like me can come off as a big philosopher. It's all about how you look at it!

t_ch: 或者，你觉得我的诡辩确实有几分道理？
t_en: Or, do you actually think my argument makes some sense?

t_ch: 话题永远离不开派对是吧？
t_en: You can't say anything without bringing up parties, can you?

t_ch: 什么派对，我刚才谈论的是艺术，是灵魂的碰撞和精神的升华！哈哈哈。
t_en: What parties? I was talking about art — the clash of souls and the ascension of spirits! Hahaha!

t_ch: 你看，只要会扯淡，我这种贪图享乐的学生也能化身大慈爱家，全看你怎么解读了。或者，你觉得我的诡辩确实有几分道理？
t_en: See? With the right spin, even a playboy like me can come off as a big philosopher. It's all about how you look at it! Or, do you actually think my argument makes some sense?

t_ch: 她讨厌我…她不讨厌我…她讨厌我…她不讨厌我……
t_en: She hates me... She likes me... She hates me... She likes me...

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼有什么办法消除一个人的记忆？你有门路吗？正规的、不正规的都行。
t_en: Do you know anyone in Penacony who can erase memories? It doesn't matter if they're reliable or not.

t_ch: 呜…我搞砸了和哈鲁小姐的初次见面…我从入学起就暗恋她了……
t_en: *sob* I messed up my first meeting with Haru. I've had a crush on her since day one.

t_ch: 她——是世间一切美好的集合，是我这种游手好闲的学术垃圾所无法企及的存在。
t_en: She... she is the epitome of everything wonderful, a beacon of grace far beyond the reach of a floundering student like me.

t_ch: 她的眼里总是映着理想，出身贫寒，双亲葬身于黑心的豆腐渣工程，却发奋苦读，誓为寰宇间穷苦的孩子们筑起安心的巢穴。
t_en: Her eyes always gleam with hope. Despite being born into poverty and losing her parents when their house collapsed due to unethical construction, she carved out a path in academia with tenacity. Now, she dreams of creating safe havens for underprivileged children.

t_ch: 更可恶的是…天呐，她还那么漂亮！！！
t_en: And to top it all off, she's just breathtakingly beautiful!

t_ch: 可惜我一直没有正式认识她的机会，直到上周，我在朋友的生日派对上遇到了她。
t_en: I hadn't had the chance to meet her until last week, when I finally ran into her at a friend's birthday party.

t_ch: 这是我第一次见她参加派对，我太紧张了，傻愣了好久，久到派对都快结束了才回过神。
t_en: It was the first time I'd seen her at a party, and I was so nervous that I just froze. I didn't snap out of it until well after the event was over.

t_ch: 你在害怕什么？
t_en: What were you so nervous about?

t_ch: 这么纯情的吗？
t_en: I didn't peg you as the romantic type.

t_ch: 因为…哈鲁小姐是不会喜欢我的。传闻哈鲁小姐讨厌关于财富学院的一切，也有人说她偏爱可爱型的男生，皮皮西人那样的。总之，我不是她的理想型。
t_en: It's because I don't stand a chance with Haru. Rumor has it she hates everything about Fortune Academy, and some say she likes cute guys. Anyway, I'm not her type.

t_ch: 我越想越紧张，一口气喝了三杯麦芽汁，想着壮壮胆向哈鲁小姐好好介绍自己。结果嘛…等我恢复意识，已经是第二天早上的事了。
t_en: Thinking I could drink to calm my growing bundle of nerves before going to talk to Haru, I downed three shots of malt juice in one go... But when I finally sobered up, I realized I'd been out cold the entire night.

t_ch: 朋友说我三杯下肚后，直挺挺地倒了下去，像块大板砖拍在了地上。这动静惊动了隔壁桌的哈鲁小姐，她以为我出了什么事，急忙跑过来……
t_en: My friends said I fell to the ground like a log after chugging down those three shots, creating so loud a thump that it startled Haru at the table next to ours. They also said she hurried over to check on me...

t_ch: 结果我当着哈鲁小姐的面，吐得稀里哗啦，像喷发的苏乐达喷泉，呕吐物洒了一地……
t_en: Only to see me retching uncontrollably, spewing my stomach contents all over the place like a SoulGlad fountain...

t_ch: 那也不能随便改别人的记忆。
t_en: That's no reason to tinker with her memory.

t_ch: 唔…可…我想消除的不是她的记忆，是我的！
t_en: Uh... I—It's not her memory I want to erase, it's mine!

t_ch: 现在她肯定对你印象深刻了。
t_en: ...At least you left a deep impression on her now.

t_ch: 是啊，所以才想把记忆消除掉…什么时候我才能忘了自己干过这种蠢事啊！
t_en: Yeah, that's exactly why I want to erase my memory of last night... Ugh, will I ever forget this hopelessly stupid blunder!?

t_ch: 我想聊聊…
t_en: I'd like to chat with you...

t_ch: 我最喜欢和人聊天了，你想从哪个话题开始？
t_en: Sure, I love chatting with people! What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 校球笼赛的预热派对？
t_en: The kick-off party for the university's Spheroid race?

t_ch: 从建校起就有的老传统了，每年正赛开始前的周末，组委会都要将大家聚在一起，为今年的赛事预热造势。
t_en: That's a tradition we've kept going since the university's founding. Every year, the organizing committee would gather everyone on the weekend before the actual race, to build up hype and excitement for the event.

t_ch: 不过不是那个「仓鼠球骑士」效力的职业球笼赛哦，是折纸大学自己的校赛。
t_en: Oh, I should clarify. This party isn't for the professional Spheroid race that Hamster Ball Knight competes in, but for Paperfold University's very own Spheroid race.

t_ch: 被邀请的不止有当年的赛车选手和杰出学生，各学院代表、校长也会出席，传闻格拉克斯校长还在第一届预热派对上喝了个酩酊大醉呢。
t_en: Other than stellar students and those participating in the race, representatives from each academy and the dean will also be invited to the party. Psst, word has it Dean Glaux got totally plastered at the inaugural kick-off party!

t_ch: 哪个学院会赢得球笼赛？
t_en: Which academy's going to win this Spheroid race?

t_ch: 必然是我们财富学院！多犹豫一秒都是对罗密欧的不尊重，他已经带领财富学院连拿两届冠军咯。
t_en: Why, Fortune Academy, of course! I have absolute faith in Romeo representing us. I won't be caught wavering, not even for a split second. And he already has two consecutive championships under his belt!

t_ch: 你问任何学生都只会得到相同的答案。每位学生只为自己的学院加油呐喊，这可是关乎学院荣耀的格拉克斯杯啊。
t_en: Ask any other student, and I'm sure you'll get the same answer. We're competing for the prestigious Glaux Cup, after all. Students are only going to cheer for their own academy.

t_ch: 你认识财富学院的契波吗？
t_en: Do you know a Czipo from Fortune Academy?

t_ch: 当然认识，那也是个自称*派对高手*的家伙。可惜啊，还是不如我。
t_en: Hmph, you bet I do. Too bad that self-proclaimed party connoisseur's nothing compared to me.

t_ch: 放着好好的球笼赛派对不来，跑去组乐队，哈，躲在教室里对着键盘噼里啪啦地乱按，能有什么意思？会比今年冒出来的那个皮皮西车手好玩吗？
t_en: Skipping the Spheroid race's party just to form a band? Bah! What fun is there in holing up in a classroom and bashing away on a keyboard? Is that going to draw any more attention than the fresh-faced Pepeshi racing this year?

t_ch: 我看啊，以他那三分钟热度的性格，坚持不了多久就要退出咯。
t_en: If you ask me, I doubt he'll stick around in that band for long. Not when he loses interest in things so quickly.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 可别和派对王客气，我一向慷慨！
t_en: As the Party King, I'm always willing to share! No need to be shy around me!

t_ch: 如果感兴趣的话，下次去我的派对吧！我欢迎任何人，即便一分钟前我才认识你。
t_en: Come to my next party if you're interested! Anyone's a guest, including those I just met.

t_ch: 「这不是哈鲁吗？正巧，我手上有两张客户送的机动球观赛门票，虽说有些廉价，但在你这里应该能发挥出价值吧。」
t_en: "Oh? It's Haru! What great timing, I happen to have two tickets to the Roboball match. I know this may sound cheap because a client gave them to me, but I'm sure it'll be of some value to you?"

t_ch: 不成，这太直接了，和我一贯的表现差太多……
t_en: No, that's coming on too strong. I hardly sound like myself...

t_ch: 你留意到这个古怪的人正在对着空气自言自语，便驻足欣赏了一会。
t_en: You see a strange guy muttering to himself, and you decide to watch him for a while.

t_ch: 那这样…「哟，瞧瞧这是谁啊？看看这两张票吧，要是你实在想要，我也不是不能…」
t_en: How about... "Oh, look who's here! I have two tickets with me. If you really want them, I guess I could—"

t_ch: ——不对！我是哪来的混蛋吗！
t_en: Gah! I sound like a total jerk!

t_ch: 他终于察觉到了你的存在，你看着他的脸由红润变得煞白，过一会又转为青绿，最后总算恢复了血色。
t_en: When he finally notices your presence, you see his face flit through a spectrum of colors: from the bright red of a flashing siren to a ghostly pallor, and to a noxious green before returning to normal.

t_ch: 你？！咳、咳哼…来咨询实习机会的吧？这个时间已经晚了，好资源早就没了，之前和你们说过，对实习要上点心…
t_en: You...!? Ack, ahem... You're here to inquire about internship opportunities, aren't you? A little too late, though. The best offers have been snapped up. Didn't I already tell you guys to put in more effort for your internships...

t_ch: 等等，你没穿校服…是游客？穿搭虽然认不出版型，但整体做工精细，莫非是私人订制？
t_en: Wait, you're not in our uniform... You're a visitor? Hmm, I don't recognize the design on your outfit, but it does boast impeccable workmanship. Could it be a custom piece?

t_ch: 垃圾桶里捡的。
t_en: I fished it out from a trashcan.

t_ch: 哈哈，简单易懂的反讽——我明白你不打算告诉我，就不再问了。
t_en: Hah, that's a deadpan tone I recognize. Alright then, I won't ask if you're not willing to share.

t_ch: 「星核猎手」牌。
t_en: A piece by Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 有意思，我猜是边远星系的高级品牌？可惜我还没入职，不然就拜托公司的前辈去联系合作了。
t_en: Oh, that's new. A luxury brand from one of the fringe planets? Too bad I'm not onboarded yet, or I'd suggest for my seniors in the IPC to work out a collaboration with them.

t_ch: 家里人给我买的。
t_en: My family got it for me.

t_ch: 哦？看来你的家人很有品味，审美水平相当不错。
t_en: Oh? I must say your family has a good eye for fashion, then.

t_ch: 不管怎么说，你成功勾起了我的兴趣。自我介绍下：我叫尚恩，担任本届财富学院的学生会主席。以后就算认识了。
t_en: Anyhow, you've gotten my interest. I'm Shawn, current president of Fortune Academy's student council. Pleased to make your acquaintance.

t_ch: 如果你有些有价值的点子，需要资金和人脉才能实现，或许我可以介绍一些合作渠道。
t_en: If you're lacking funds or the right network for a brilliant idea, I may just be the person to link you up with a partner or opportunity.

t_ch: （提出你的星际垃圾场计划）
t_en: (Tell him your plan for the interastral junkyard.)

t_ch: 不行，难度太大。垃圾分类的标准就和宇宙里的种族一样繁杂，很难统一。
t_en: Hardly feasible. There are as many ways to categorize trash as we have species in the universe. It's not going to be easy to come up with a common standard.

t_ch: （希望承接恋爱咨询业务）
t_en: (Tell him your interest in starting a love consultancy.)

t_ch: 想法很好，但按我的理解…钱，买不来，爱情。
t_en: That's a novel idea, but from how I see it... money isn't going to buy anyone love.

t_ch: …不，可能只是因为我还不够有钱？
t_en: ...Wait, maybe I just don't have enough money yet?

t_ch: 三块钱收十块钱，无限收。
t_en: Fiver for your tenner. This deal's always open.

t_ch: 你左脚踩右脚上天算了。
t_en: Pfft, you're not trapping anyone with that circular reasoning.

t_ch: 唉，就没有比那个建房子的傻梦想更靠谱的提案吗……
t_en: *sigh* Is that silly dream of building buildings really the most promising proposition out there...?

t_ch: 记着——把握机遇，赢得人生。
t_en: Remember this: Make the most out of every opportunity to win in life.

t_ch: 若是要跻身商界前列，与上流人士的联系必不可少。在交流中，那些人的知识与思维方式会自然为你所有。
t_en: You're going to have to interact with the highborn and the elite if you want to make it to the top in the business scene, and when you do, you'll naturally pick up the way they think.

t_ch: 相对地，不要在无价值的人身上浪费太多时间，公司的优秀实习生都深谙此道。
t_en: The same goes for the converse group. You don't want to waste too much of your time on people who are of no value. Every well performing IPC intern lives by this guideline.

t_ch: 货币…人脉…对无价之物而言，又有什么意义呢？
t_en: Wealth and networks... How do they compare to things that are truly invaluable?

t_ch: 我当然清楚，自己在其他人眼里是什么评价。
t_en: Sure, I know how others think of me.

t_ch: 「活得一帆风顺的家伙」、「跋扈阔少」、「冷血无情的逐利者」……
t_en: The guy with the smooth-sailing life, the rich and bossy guy, the self-serving profiteer...

t_ch: 但他们怎么明白？所有朋友都只记得你的家庭，却对你本人的努力与成就不屑一顾，多么悲哀啊。
t_en: I don't expect them to look past any of that. People around you only bother remembering your background, not your efforts or achievements. Sad, huh?

t_ch: 而那些虚名能带来什么？信用点吗？就连她的应允都买不来……
t_en: And what can this weighty name bring me? Credits? What use is it when I can't even get her to go out with me...

t_ch: 原来你是真喜欢那位啊？
t_en: So, you're serious about her?

t_ch: 还好你有自知之明。
t_en: At least you have some self-awareness.

t_ch: …唉，你不知道，她是筑梦学院的优等生，是个心思纯粹的设计师…商人的天敌！
t_en: ... *sigh* You won't understand. She's a model student at Dreamweaver Academy, an altruistic architect with a heart of gold... The archenemy of us entrepreneurs!

t_ch: 在她眼里，我所拥有的财富和人脉，包括我的处事方式在内，恐怕都和苏乐达公司的股价一样与她毫无关联。
t_en: My wealth, social connections, and even my approach to things probably mean as much as SoulGlad's shares to her — irrelevant and nothing at all.

t_ch: 我…倾心于她的率真。但或许，我是在羡慕她的无忧无虑…唉……
t_en: I... like how she lives true to herself. Or maybe I'm just envious of how carefree she is... *sigh*

t_ch: 我当然清楚，自己在其他人眼里是什么评价。
t_en: Sure, I know how others think of me.

t_ch: 「活得一帆风顺的家伙」、「跋扈阔少」、「冷血无情的逐利者」……
t_en: The guy with the smooth-sailing life, the rich and bossy guy, the self-serving profiteer...

t_ch: 但他们怎么明白？所有朋友都只记得你的家庭，却对你本人的努力与成就不屑一顾，多么悲哀啊。
t_en: I don't expect them to look past any of that. People around you only bother remembering your background, not your efforts or achievements. Sad, huh?

t_ch: 而那些虚名能带来什么？信用点吗？就连她的应允都买不来……
t_en: And what can this weighty name bring me? Credits? What use is it when I can't even get her to go out with me...

t_ch: 商机无处不在，而我能听见它们的声音。
t_en: Business opportunities are around every corner, and I can hear them calling out to me.

t_ch: …你觉得什么是成功的生意？把食物卖给饿肚子的人，把药品卖给濒死的病人？
t_en: ...What's a successful business to you? Is it selling food to those who are hungry? Or selling medicines to those on the brink of death?

t_ch: 这种商机谁都能想到。而在匹诺康尼，所有人都知道的赛道自然会遇到无数竞争者。
t_en: These are common business ideas anyone can come up with. And if you venture into such trades here on Penacony, you'll only find yourself up against a stampede of competitors.

t_ch: 这次校庆，很多人看中了那些外来访客的流量，准备大赚一笔…殊不知从根本上就错了！
t_en: Many are counting on the influx of visitors to make a killing at our university's upcoming anniversary, but little do they know that those tourists aren't their cash cows!

t_ch: 我提供的商品，是他们堆放货物的仓库！不论竞争多么激烈，这笔生意绝对稳赚不赔！
t_en: I, on the other hand, am offering them storage space for their goods! This business idea is sure to rake in profit, no matter how intense the competition gets!

t_ch: 也正因为我把握住了机会，公司的实习录用书才会理所当然地摆在我的桌子上。
t_en: And that's how I easily earned an internship offer at the IPC. Because I spotted an untapped business opportunity and jumped in with the right service offering.

t_ch: 这就是精英与普通人的差距——财富学院的那些人得好好学着点。
t_en: This is what separates the cream of the crop from the rest. Something those Fortune Academy students ought to learn.

t_ch: 在学校听课就不算荒废时间？想得太简单了。
t_en: If you don't find sitting in class a total waste of time, then you haven't seen the real world.

t_ch: 我还想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to continue chatting with you.

t_ch: 行，别太久，我的时间很宝贵。
t_en: Sure, but let's not drag it out too long. My time is money, after all.

t_ch: 你都有哪些渠道？
t_en: Tell me about your business connections?

t_ch: 呵，我的人脉好些都深居幕后，恐怕报出名字来你也不见得认识。但既然你诚心发问，我就勉为其难地举个你肯定知道的例子。
t_en: Hah, many of my business associates are bigwigs who work behind the scenes, so people you're not likely to have heard of. But since you're interested, I guess I can name someone you're sure to know?

t_ch: 苜蓿草家系现任家主，奥帝•艾弗法先生。我和他有过几次愉快的同席，彼此相谈甚欢。
t_en: Mr. Oti Alfalfa, the current head of the Alfalfa Family. We were both at the same events a couple of times, and I had a few pleasant exchanges with him.

t_ch: 理解了吗？至于其他…如果想知道细节，等你向我展示过你的价值后再谈。
t_en: Have an idea of my business network now? You're going to have to show me your worth if you want to learn more, though the discretion is still mine.

t_ch: 你喜欢那位「哈鲁」？
t_en: You like that Haru girl, don't you?

t_ch: 我没…！…这是商业机密，想打听得付钱。
t_en: I don't—! ...That's a trade secret that will cost you money.

t_ch: 那我去找当事人问问好了。
t_en: I guess I'll ask her myself, then.

t_ch: 「哟，看看这两张票…」
t_en: "Oh, I have two tickets with me..."

t_ch: …行，算我求你，可千万别抖出去。
t_en: ...Fine, I'll tell you. But you have to keep it a secret.

t_ch: 她是筑梦学院的优秀学生，边远星球来的贫困户，背景薄得吓人，用大号字体都印不满一张纸…
t_en: She's a model student at Dreamweaver Academy, but she comes from a poor family from one of those faraway planets. She hardly has any background to speak of. Whatever information found on her doesn't take up a page, even with a <unbreak>20</unbreak>-point font...

t_ch: 别误会，我没有窥探别人隐私的兴趣，但我也没法阻止家里那些多事的保镖自顾自地做了全面调查。
t_en: No, I'm not one to snoop around others' private information, but there's nothing I can do to stop those nosey bodyguards employed by my family from running a thorough investigation on her.

t_ch: 天真的女孩，支撑她的梦想就和她的出身一样单纯，但…我欣赏她的才华。
t_en: She's just a guileless girl keeping her dream alive with a passion as bare as her background, but... it's her talent that attracted me.

t_ch: 你的演技不错。
t_en: You spin a great tale.

t_ch: 哼，我就当没听懂你的挖苦了，只论演技，那是自然。
t_en: Hmph, I'll pretend I didn't hear that sarcasm. Of course I'm a great actor.

t_ch: 以财富学院学生会主席的名义，我可以明确告诉你。实习、面试、商务洽谈，不管哪个地方都用得上这门技术。
t_en: I don't mind telling you this as president of Fortune Academy's student council: People are always portraying a certain image, whether at internships, interviews, or business negotiations.

t_ch: 正因如此，做好事前演练，就是提升商业活动成功率最有效的方式。
t_en: That's why rehearsing beforehand is the best practice for acing any business function you're expected at.

t_ch: 你那是什么表情？行，我承认…排练的地点最好也该事先确认一下。
t_en: ...What's with that look? Fine, I'll admit it. It's not just rehearsing beforehand, but rehearsing beforehand at the right place.

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 行，如果你想到了好点子，记得来找我。
t_en: Alright. Come look for me when you get a good business idea.

t_ch: 银河经济、星际市场导论、跨界金融管理…学院的课程安排听起来很高大上，可实际呢？
t_en: Cosmic Economy, Introduction to Interastral Markets, Cross-Border Financial Management... Yeah, the academy sure put together a curriculum that sounds downright impressive. But what do you really get from those classes?

t_ch: 全是纸上谈兵！在你实习的第一天，这些知识就会统统都被推翻！
t_en: Theories that won't work in the real world! Everything you learned in class will be proven wrong on your very first day as an intern!

t_ch: 计划永远赶不上变化，想要真正看清未来趋势、掌控市场变量，你至少得有个天才那样的头脑——可我要真是天才，还用得着上课吗？
t_en: Nothing ever goes entirely as forecasted, so you'll need to have the mental capacity of a genius to astutely pick up on emerging trends and navigate market variables. Then we're back to the question again: Is there a point to sitting in class if you're already a genius?

t_ch: 还不如把学费交给街边的逐梦客！说不定能给你带来点商机和人脉！
t_en: You're better off paying your tuition fees to those hustling Dreamchasers instead! They might just offer you a business opportunity or become your business partner!

t_ch: 唉，身为学生会主席，我已经向学校提过无数次，要扩大实习经历在绩点中的占比，却被当成是欺负贫困学生而数次被警告。
t_en: *sigh* I'm only doing my job as president of the student council, submitting suggestion after suggestion to increase the weightage of the internship module in our overall GPA, but all I get in return are warnings from the university! For discriminating against students from less well-off backgrounds!

t_ch: 真是荒谬…如果有人问我推荐哪门课，我一定要举个大牌子告诉他：「快逃」！
t_en: As if they aren't absurd enough already... If anyone comes asking for my recommendation on which classes to take, you bet I'll wave a giant "DROP OUT NOW" placard in their face!

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 行，如果你想到了好点子，记得来找我。
t_en: Alright. Come look for me when you get a good business idea.

t_ch: 读过最新的《折纸时报》了吗？猜你就没有，我念给你听——
t_en: Have you read the latest issue of the Paperfold Times? I imagine not. Let me read it for you...

t_ch: 「随着第<unbreak>550</unbreak>届梦境大学生球笼竞速预选赛落幕，折纸大学终于迎来了历史上第一位皮皮西首席车手。」
t_en: "As the <unbreak>550</unbreak>th Dreamscape Collegiate Spheroid Race Qualifiers come to an end, Paperfold University finally welcomes their first ever Pepeshi lead driver in history."

t_ch: 「来自谐乐学院二年级的普蕾玛势不可挡，上周末以一场华丽的胜利，拿下预选赛积分第一。她将同其他三位车手一齐，代表谐乐学院出战今年的格拉克斯杯。」
t_en: "Prema, a second-year student at Charmony Academy, proved to be unstoppable, taking a glorious victory over the weekend and securing the highest score in qualifying. She will join three other drivers to represent Charmony Academy and battle for this year's Glaux Cup."

t_ch: 「她的球笼『飞翔的皮皮西号』在枪响后一骑绝尘，这可是令全场车手嫉妒的顶尖之作，不难想象她背后赞助商对其不计成本的投入。」
t_en: "Her Spheroid, The Flying Pepeshi, left all of her competitors in its dust from the moment the starting gun was fired. It is a masterpiece of craftsmanship that has inspired envy in all of her fellow drivers. It is clear that her sponsors have spared no cost in their investment towards her and her future."

t_ch: 「她相较其他车手稍显迷你的身躯也令车迷们万分揪心，我们衷心希望普蕾玛在长达二十四系统时的拉力赛中不会陷入脱水危险，现在开始增重是她最好的选择。」
t_en: "Her smaller build compared to those of other drivers has become a concern for racing fans. We sincerely hope that Prema will not be at risk of dehydration during the <unbreak>24</unbreak>-system hour rally ahead. We believe her best choice would be to start gaining some weight."

t_ch: 这篇报道，你读起来什么感觉？
t_en: What do you feel when you read this article?

t_ch: 他们在关心你。
t_en: They are showing concern for you.

t_ch: 他们阴阳怪气你。
t_en: They are being sarcastic about you.

t_ch: 我不做评价。
t_en: No comment.

t_ch: 皮皮西…皮皮西…皮皮西的一切都是因为皮皮西…而不是因为我的努力……
t_en: Pepeshi... Pepeshi... Pepeshi... It's all because I'm a Pepeshi... It's not because of my own hard work at all...

t_ch: 皮皮西的体能很弱无法支撑球笼赛强度…皮皮西能成首席是赞助商看中了球笼赛运动在皮皮西市场的发展潜力……
t_en: Pepeshi are too physically weak to handle the intensity of a Spheroid Race... A Pepeshi was only able to become the lead driver because sponsors took an interest in the potential development of Spheroid sports in the Pepeshi market...

t_ch: 皮皮西他，皮皮西她，皮皮西所有人！！！
t_en: Pepeshi this, Pepeshi that, Pepeshi everything!!!

t_ch: 皮皮西！皮皮西！皮皮西！
t_en: Pepeshi! Pepeshi! Pepeshi!

t_ch: 但你确实是皮皮西人。
t_en: But you ARE a Pepeshi...

t_ch: 哼，让他们议论去吧，闹得越轰烈越好，越多人关注越好，然后我会证明给所有人看！
t_en: Humph! Let them talk! The more fuss they make and the more people take notice, the better! Then, I will prove them all wrong!

t_ch: 我还想和你聊聊
t_en: Can we talk a little more?

t_ch: 行啊，只要你不是冲着皮皮西人来的就好。
t_en: Alright, as long as you're not obsessed with the whole Pepeshi thing, too.

t_ch: 各学院的车手都是什么风格？
t_en: What are the drivers of each academy like?

t_ch: 球笼赛可不只是车手间的较量，它更是考验团队的综合作战。每个学院都有自己的优势与策略。
t_en: The Spheroid racing contest is not just a contest between drivers, it is more of a comprehensive battle that tests the entire team. Each academy has their own strengths and strategies.

t_ch: 说说筑梦学院？
t_en: Tell me about Dreamweaver Academy?

t_ch: 首先，你需要一辆出色的球笼车。格拉克斯杯的赛道复杂多变，球笼车需要在多种类型的赛道稳定发挥性能。论造车，没有人拼得过筑梦学院那群学霸们。
t_en: First, you need an outstanding Spheroid. The tracks for the Glaux Cup are complex and varied, Spheroids need to be able to perform reliably on all kinds of tracks. When it comes to Spheroid construction, no one can compete with the Dreamweaver Academy bookworms.

t_ch: 说说财富学院？
t_en: Tell me about Fortune Academy?

t_ch: 获得冠军的最重要的因素——钱。球笼赛是匹诺康尼最昂贵的运动，造车需要钱，修建训练场地、更换训练耗材需要更多的钱。
t_en: The most important factor when it comes to taking victory... is money. Spheroid racing is the most expensive sport in Penacony. Creating a Spheroid costs money, but building a training ground and replacing supplies used in training costs even more.

t_ch: 不得不说，没有人比财富学院的家伙们更会搞钱——投资收益、拉赞助…靠着这些钞能力，他们已经连续拿下两届冠军了。
t_en: I have to say, no one manages to accumulate money like those guys at Fortune Academy... Interest from investments, attracting sponsors... They have managed to take two championships in a row with these shrewd money-making skills.

t_ch: 说说谐乐学院？
t_en: Tell me about Charmony Academy?

t_ch: 车手必须得有过硬的心理素质和信仰，以撑过极端的比赛环境——长达二十四系统时的拉力赛、失重、十倍重力…多样的考验。
t_en: Drivers need outstanding psychological parameters and faith in their own capabilities. That's what it takes to navigate the extreme racing environment. The <unbreak>24</unbreak>-system hour rally, weightlessness, tenfold gravity... All sorts of trials await.

t_ch: 你看我的心理素质就知道了，咱们谐乐学院盛产优秀车手，大致是得益于我们对信仰之事有一种忘我式的虔诚。
t_en: As you can see from my mentality, Charmony Academy creates outstanding drivers. This is most likely thanks to our selfless devotion to concepts such as belief and faith.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 三种致胜的关键因素，缺一不可，没有哪个学院能垄断比赛的胜利，我们用自己的所长彼此制衡，形成了当下三足鼎立的平衡局面。
t_en: These are the three crucial factors for victory that are all indispensable. This way, no one academy can monopolize victory. We each use our own strengths to check and balance each other, which is why we now have this current state of three-legged equilibrium.

t_ch: 哪个学院会赢得球笼赛？
t_en: Which academy's going to win this Spheroid race?

t_ch: 问谐乐学院首席车手这个问题？认真的？
t_en: You're asking the lead driver of Charmony Academy this question? Are you serious?

t_ch: 你撤回吧。
t_en: Take it back.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 你要是撞见《折纸时报》的那个小编，打我电话，我马上到！
t_en: If you come across that Paperfold Times editor, give me a call, and I'll be there in a jiffy!

t_ch: 可以摸摸你头上的绒球吗？
t_en: Can I touch the fluff ball on your head?

t_ch: …我就知道你要问这个！别以为皮皮西人的体型小，就可以随便欺负！
t_en: ...I knew you were going to ask that! Don't think that you can just bully Pepeshi because we're small!

t_ch: 再说这个绒球有什么特别吗，就因为长在皮皮西人的头上，才让你这么感兴趣？
t_en: What is so special about this fluff ball anyway? Is it just the fact that they grow on our heads makes you so obsessed with them?

t_ch: 皮皮西…皮皮西…到处都对皮皮西人另眼相待，我真是受够了！
t_en: Pepeshi... Pepeshi... Everyone treats us so differently just because we're Pepeshi. I've had enough!

t_ch: 我没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 你要是撞见《折纸时报》的那个小编，打我电话，我马上到！
t_en: If you come across that Paperfold Times editor, give me a call, and I'll be there in a jiffy!

t_ch: 我们皮皮西人只是…浓缩了一点，又不是没手没脚！
t_en: We Pepeshi are just... We're just a little condensed. We're not helpless!

t_ch: 皮皮西是一个德智体美全面发展的种族，我们并不只有脑子好。
t_en: The Pepeshi people are developed in morality, intelligence, physicality, and beauty! We are more than just clever.

t_ch: 只是…我们和所谓的标准体型生物体育竞技时，常常落下劣势，他们跑一步，我们要跨两步，有时候是三步。
t_en: It's just... When it comes to physical competition against so-called standard-sized organisms, we are often at a disadvantage — when they run one step, we have to take two, sometimes three.

t_ch: 输多了，皮皮西人也失去了信心，开始逃避参与体育竞技项目。
t_en: After many losses, the Pepeshi lost their confidence and began to avoid taking part in physical competitions.

t_ch: 但没想到吧，哼哼，赛车运动中，更小的身形意味着更小的负重，就等于更快的速度！
t_en: But I bet you never imagined, hehe, that when it comes to racing, a smaller body means a lighter load for the vehicle, meaning greater speed!

t_ch: 唯一要战胜的敌人，只有我们自己！
t_en: The only rival that can defeat us is ourselves!

t_ch: 我要真有他们说得那么神乎其神，还在这开球笼车？
t_en: If I really were as exceptional as they say, would I still be racing Spheroids here?

t_ch: 校园里的小报写手格外偏爱我的坐骑。自从我跑出了点成绩，他们从未间断过猜测它的来历，各式各样传言都有——
t_en: The campus reporters just have a bias towards my Spheroid. Ever since I started to get results, they have not stopped speculating about its origins. I've heard all kinds of rumors...

t_ch: 有说，这车是苜蓿草家系赞助的，他们看中了皮皮西待开发的体育市场，于是斥巨资请来博识学会星际动力学派的专家设计了「飞翔的皮皮西号」。
t_en: Some say that it came from an Alfalfa Family sponsor — that they saw the potential in the developing Pepeshi sporting market and gave the Intelligentsia Guild's Interastral School of Kinetics a huge stipend to design The Flying Pepeshi.

t_ch: 我还是更喜欢另一个版本：这辆球笼搭载了忆质作为动力源，那是过去散溢忆质的残存，被皮皮西人私藏，观众目睹的胜利不过是忆质燃烧时产生的集体梦中梦。
t_en: There's another version I like even more, though: That my Spheroid is equipped with memoria as a power source. It is a secret stash of remnant memoria that was gathered by the Pepeshi. They say that the victory that the spectators witnessed was nothing more than a dream within a dream created by the combustion of memoria.

t_ch: 其实我的球笼只是最普通的型号喔，与学校的练习用车别无二致，只是舱内被改装得更适合我的个头，外表用了荧光粉的涂装，仅此而已。
t_en: Actually, my Spheroid is a completely normal model. It's exactly the same as the campus training model. The interior is just adjusted to suit my size better and the exterior has been painted fluorescent. That's all.

t_ch: 我和其他选手一样敬畏公平竞技的原则：参赛守则第<unbreak>521</unbreak>条——
t_en: I respect the principle of fair competition just like any of my fellow competitors: See article <unbreak>521</unbreak> of the competition guidelines...

t_ch: 禁止折纸大学校外人员或企业以任何形式参与比赛用车的设计、改造、制作，驾驶违规车辆的选手将被取消正赛资格，终生禁赛！
t_en: It is forbidden for personnel or businesses from outside of Paperfold University to take part in the design, modification, creation, or driving of competition vehicles. Any contestants found to be driving illegal cars will be disqualified from the current competition and banned for life!

t_ch: 真心难寻也无妨，我会好好利用你们的虚情假意。
t_en: Though you sound just as insincere as everyone else, I will make good use of your false wishes.

t_ch: 别看我身边簇满了人，商人、星探、经纪人、赞助商…他们没一个真心相信我的。
t_en: Don't be fooled by the traders, talent scouts, agents, and sponsors gathering around me... None of them actually believe in my capabilities.

t_ch: 他们都清楚怎么利用我这样的人——皮皮西人，有点天赋，有些成绩，非常冲动，有些孤立无援。
t_en: They know full well how to make use of someone like me... I'm a Pepeshi. I've got a little skill and some success. I'm impulsive and slightly isolated...

t_ch: 我被选为谐乐学院的球笼车手，参加预选赛时，这群人就找上我了。
t_en: It was when I was chosen to become the Charmony Academy Spheroid driver and took part in qualifying that they came to me.

t_ch: 刚开始，一切都很美妙。他们带我去梦境里最好的餐厅，递上完美的商业计划，然后不断地向我灌输梦想：我会是第一个皮皮西冠军，成为所有皮皮西人的榜样……
t_en: At the beginning, it was all wonderful. They took me to the finest restaurants in the Dreamscape, laid out perfect business plans, and kept filling my head with dreams: I would be the first Pepeshi champion, an idol for all Pepeshi...

t_ch: 但我清楚，如果我在亮相的首场比赛内没有惊艳全场，他们就会抛弃我，转而寻找下一个商机。
t_en: But I knew full well that if I hadn't shocked everyone with my debut performance, they'd have abandoned me and gone straight after their next business opportunity.

t_ch: 别误会了，我愿意配合他们的演出，更要感谢他们把我推到聚光灯下——
t_en: Don't get me wrong. I am happy to play along with their little performance. I'm actually grateful for them pushing me into the spotlight...

t_ch: 只要我拿下冠军，只要我进职业联赛，只要我能一呼百应，我就能…就能成为他们口中的那个希望！
t_en: If I can just win the championship and enter the pro leagues, if I can just win everyone's favor, then... then I can become the hope they all talk about!

t_ch: 只要有梦想，皮皮西人也可以被仰望！
t_en: As long as I have a dream, people will look up to Pepeshi as well!

t_ch: 时间过得好快。去年的这个时候，我以最后一名的成绩在预选赛中落败，不过那天也是我命运的转折点……
t_en: Time really flies. This time last year I was knocked out of the qualifiers, finishing in last place. Although, that was when my fate turned around...

t_ch: 不公的境遇常常是结构性的，市面现有的球笼没有适合皮皮西人的尺寸。赛前我也四处央求同学帮我改装，但没有人愿意把时间浪费在一个皮皮西选手身上。
t_en: Injustice is often structural, and none of the Spheroids on the market are fitted to a Pepeshi's body size. I begged my classmates to help me modify it before the competition, but no one was willing to waste their time on a Pepeshi contestant.

t_ch: 无奈之下，我只能开着过于宽敞的球笼车参赛……
t_en: With nowhere to turn, I had no choice but to drive an oversized Spheroid in the competition.

t_ch: 你见过搞笑卡通片里，身高不够的小孩怎么开车吗？我就是那样完成比赛的——屁股下垫着三本百科全书，腿上绑着一根延长的木棍用来踩油门。
t_en: You've seen how children drive cars in cartoons when they're too short, right? That's how I was in the contest... I had three encyclopedias under me as a cushion and a wooden stick strapped to my leg to help me step on the gas pedal.

t_ch: 结果，连带孙子来玩的老教授开得都比我快，还快了整整半圈。很遗憾吧，对那些本就瞧不起皮皮西的人，我的失败又巩固了他们心中的偏见。
t_en: In the end, even an old professor who had brought his grandson along for a fun ride drove faster than me. They beat me by half a lap! It's a shame, right? I failed and only reinforced the views of those people who already looked down on Pepeshi.

t_ch: 只有一个人不这么想——那天他看了我的比赛，认为眼中所见并非狼藉，而是一颗冉冉升起的明日之星。
t_en: There was just one person that did not agree... That day, he watched my race and believed that it was not a disaster he was witnessing, but the rising of a star.

t_ch: 赛后，他让赛训团队把我的驾驶舱改成了适合皮皮西人体型的构造，这才有了后面的故事…他就是上届球笼赛的冠军，财富学院的罗密欧。
t_en: Then, he had the training team convert my cockpit to suit a Pepeshi's size, and that's how we got to where we are today... He was actually the champion of the last Spheroid contest, Romeo of Fortune Academy.

t_ch: 可你是谐乐学院的啊…
t_en: But, you're from Charmony Academy...

t_ch: 他打算背刺财富学院？
t_en: He's going to betray Fortune Academy?

t_ch: …还有这种好事？
t_en: ...People are actually that kind?

t_ch: 虽然球笼赛事关学院荣誉，但我们更珍视梦想。别看赛场上各个学院争得你死我活，私下我们经常交流经验，训练结束后都会聚在一起喝麦芽果汁呢。
t_en: Although the Spheroid Race is a matter of academic honor, we are more focused on our own dreams. On the track, the academies fight tooth and nail, but behind the scenes, we frequently share our experiences and often get together for malt juice after training.

t_ch: 这一次，我们会代表各自学院的荣誉而战——我绝对不会输给他！
t_en: This time, we will battle for the honor of our respective academies... I will not lose to him!

t_ch: ——那家伙真是气死人！下次再让我见着，非得给他来个过肩摔不可。
t_en: ...That guy just makes me so dang mad! Next time I see him, I'm gonna judo throw him into next week!

t_ch: 眼前的学生怒气冲冲，你开始犹豫起是否该继续搭话。
t_en: The student ahead of you is boiling with rage, you start to wonder if talking to them is a good idea.

t_ch: 校园里禁止使用暴力。
t_en: Violence is not allowed on campus.

t_ch: 哼，反正在梦里没事！没把他踹到苏乐达池子里都算好的了！
t_en: Humph, it doesn't matter, we're in a dream! He'll be lucky if I don't boot him into the SoulGlad pond!

t_ch: 可以再接个飞踢！
t_en: Then give him a flying kick!

t_ch: 对，往胸口踢！想到那花里胡哨的领带我就来气！
t_en: Yeah, right in the chest! Just thinking about him and his fancy ties drives me mad!

t_ch: 你们两个的反应还真搭……
t_en: You two are so similar...

t_ch: 谁谁谁，谁和那种玩意搭！
t_en: Wh—Who would want to be compared to someone like that!?

t_ch: …呃，啊…对、对不起！让你看到丢人的事了……
t_en: ...Uhh, ah... S—Sorry! Sorry you had to see me like that...

t_ch: 你好！是游客对吧！请问你对展览感兴趣吗？筑梦学院新一届概念艺术展再过几个月就要举办了。如果有时间，请务必来参观！
t_en: Hello! You must be a visitor! Are you interested in exhibitions? Dreamweaver Academy's newest conceptual art exhibition is going to be taking place in a few months. If you have time, you must come and check it out!

t_ch: 虽然参展的大部分都是学生作品…但我们学院的教学质量可是声名远扬！
t_en: Although the majority of our displays are going to be student works... The quality of our school's education is known far and wide!

t_ch: 另外，包括门票和展品交易在内，这次展会收入的九成都会捐赠给星际知名的慈善机构。手头宽裕的话，还请为银河里贫困的人们贡献一份心意！
t_en: Also, <unbreak>90%</unbreak> of the proceeds taken from exhibition tickets and trades are going to be donated to famous interastral charitable organizations. If you are feeling generous, please consider making a donation to those less fortunate in the cosmos!

t_ch: 你变得可真快。
t_en: Your mood changed quickly.

t_ch: 哈哈…唉…不行，我还是咽不下这口气。找个时间，得把那家伙捆起来扔垃圾桶里去！
t_en: Haha... Ah... No, I just can't get this guy off my mind. Next time I see him, I'm tying him up and throwing him in the trashcan!

t_ch: 能聊聊吗？
t_en: Can we talk?

t_ch: 当然，请随便问。我很乐意提供解答！
t_en: Of course, ask whatever you like. I would be happy to answer your questions!

t_ch: 你为什么想要当志愿者呢？
t_en: Why have you chosen to volunteer?

t_ch: 支持慈善事业一直是我的梦想，并且我自己的作品也会参展。既然宣传工作缺少人手，何乐而不为呢？
t_en: It has always been my dream to support charitable organizations. Plus, my works are going to be displayed, too. As we didn't have enough people doing the promotion work, I figured why not get involved?

t_ch: …其实，还有一个原因。我已经到了该申请实习的时候，但简历上能写的实在是有些单薄。
t_en: ...Actually, there is another reason. It's time to start applying to internships, but I've realized that my resume is looking a little thin.

t_ch: 那家伙一直也拿这事嘲笑我，虽然很不愿承认，但他说的确实有道理。
t_en: That guy kept mocking me for this. Even though I hate to admit it, he may be right.

t_ch: 只靠学院的成绩，恐怕竞争力不太够，我需要志愿者的经历来填补简历空白。
t_en: Just relying on my academic grades alone will not give me enough of a competitive edge. I need to fill the blanks with volunteer work.

t_ch: 要不别卷了吧？
t_en: Why don't you give up?

t_ch: 你可以把字体改大点。
t_en: You could just make the text bigger?

t_ch: 你是没见过我那些同学的简历，每一份看起来都像在闪闪发光。举几个例子，像是——「蝉联七十二届匹诺康尼历史知识竞赛冠军」……
t_en: You've never seen my classmates' resumes. They almost look like they glow! Let me give you some examples... "Champion of the Penaconian History Competition for seventy-two consecutive years..."

t_ch: 「经营品牌企业，职员由自己的一百五十名管家来担任。」
t_en: "Manages a brand enterprise staffed by <unbreak>150</unbreak> personal butlers."

t_ch: 「当本人二十五岁时，已经拥有了长达三十二年的工作经验。」
t_en: "By the age of <unbreak>25</unbreak>, had already amassed <unbreak>32</unbreak> years of work experience."

t_ch: 相比之下，我的「品德良好，成绩优异」看起来就和没气的苏乐达一样没劲。
t_en: Compared to them, my "outstanding work ethic and excellent grades" is like a bottle of flat SoulGlad.

t_ch: 展会上会有哪些作品？
t_en: What kind of works will there be at the exhibition?

t_ch: 我想想，今年同学们的想法相对保守一些，多半都是最小立体迷宫、微观园林之类的创作。
t_en: Let me think. This year's students' ideas are a little conservative. It will be mostly minimalist 3D mazes and microscopic gardens.

t_ch: 但相比于往届，展品形式有了一定程度的拓展，比如受关注的动物体验梦泡，迷因表演等等。当然，展馆本身也是一件精妙的作品。
t_en: Although, the forms of exhibitions will be expanded somewhat compared with previous years. There will be trending animal-experience dream bubbles, meme performances, and other things... Of course, the exhibition hall itself is an outstanding work.

t_ch: 那个惹了你的「家伙」是？
t_en: Who is this "guy" that made you so mad?

t_ch: 唉，其实是我自己的事。你要是真好奇…行吧，能跟人讲讲也好。
t_en: Ah, that's just a personal issue. Although, if you're really curious... Alright, I guess it's good to talk to people about these things.

t_ch: ——财富学院的学生会主席，尚恩。那家伙就是个行走的投资机器。
t_en: ...Chairman of the Fortune Academy student council — Shawn. That guy is a walking investment machine.

t_ch: 唯利是图，小家子气。就因为我前些日子跟他起了冲突，就开始处处找我、甚至找整个筑梦学院的麻烦！
t_en: He is a petty man who only cares about profit. Ever since I got into a little dispute with him, he's been causing trouble for both me and everyone in Dreamweaver Academy!

t_ch: 这次也是，把展会需要用的器材都提前买断了，害得我们没法开始筹备！
t_en: This time is no different. He bought out all of the equipment we were going to need for the exhibition in advance so we couldn't begin our preparations!

t_ch: 还故意在我眼前晃，把产权证摆给我看！说什么「喂，我知道你需要它」…要换成你，想不想揍他？
t_en: He deliberately rubbed it in my face and showed off his proof of purchase. He kept saying: "Hey, I know you need these." If it were you, would you not want to hit him?

t_ch: 懂了。
t_en: I get it.

t_ch: 是得下狠手。
t_en: He needs a good whoopin'.

t_ch: 对吧！那家伙明明脑子挺好使，怎么就这么小心眼？不能把心思用在正道上吗？！
t_en: Right!? He's clearly a smart guy. How can he be so hung up on such petty things? Can he not just put his mind to good use!?

t_ch: 好在他还没坏得那么彻底，后来答应便宜租给我们，最后造成的损失…就只有让我在游客面前情绪失控而已。
t_en: Fortunately, he wasn't completely unreasonable and agreed to rent them to us at a low price. In the end, the only damage he caused... was causing me to lose my temper in front of visitors.

t_ch: 真是糟透了，你要是愿意帮我个忙，就当没听过这茬吧。
t_en: He really is awful. But, please do me a favor and pretend I never told you this.

t_ch: 我没什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 有空的话，请务必来参观筑梦学院的概念艺术展！
t_en: If you get time, please come along to the Dreamweaver Academy Conceptual Art Exhibition!

t_ch: 我没什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 有空的话，请务必来参观筑梦学院的概念艺术展！
t_en: If you get time, please come along to the Dreamweaver Academy Conceptual Art Exhibition!

t_ch: 又见面了，游客！还是对我们的展览感兴趣吗？
t_en: We meet again, visitor! Are you still interested in our exhibition?

t_ch: 这次的展览，从收集作品到展馆规划，我们都花了很多功夫。虽然中间产生了点小插曲，但…无关紧要，哈哈。
t_en: We have put a lot of work into collecting display pieces and planning how the hall would be laid out. We had a little trouble along the way, but... It's nothing serious, haha.

t_ch: 重要的是，这些作品会转换为价值，而这份价值…能够切实地帮上其他人。
t_en: What's important is that these pieces will be converted into value, and this value... will be able to really help others.

t_ch: 对筑梦的热情，以及对相关课程的掌握，我有自信不会输给任何人。
t_en: I am confident that my passion for dreamweaving and my grasp of the curriculum are unmatched!

t_ch: 告诉你个秘密吧…我的家乡非常贫穷，和富丽堂皇的梦境截然不同。在那里，即使有家庭的庇护，寻找一个能遮风挡雨的稳定场所仍然要拼尽全力。
t_en: I'll tell you a secret... My hometown is extremely poor. It is a stark contrast to the rich and extravagant Dreamscape. There, even if you have a family to protect you, finding a stable place to shelter from the wind and rain takes real dedication.

t_ch: 离开残破的废墟，住进完整结实的建筑…看起来理所当然的事，对我们而言却极为困难。
t_en: Leaving the dilapidated ruins and moving into a complete and sturdy building... Though it seems like such a simple thing, it was extremely difficult for us.

t_ch: 所以我从小就定下目标——要为家乡的人们建造足够多坚固的房屋，供所有人安身。
t_en: So, I set myself an objective at a young age... I would construct enough strong and sturdy buildings in my hometown to give everyone a place to live.

t_ch: 这也是我选择进入筑梦学院的原因…哈哈，虽然学到的东西和这个完全不搭边……
t_en: This is also why I chose to come to Dreamweaver Academy... Haha, although everything I learn here is completely unrelated...

t_ch: 不过，对筑梦的热情，以及对相关课程的掌握，我有自信不会输给任何人。
t_en: But I'm confident that my passion for dreamweaving and my grasp of the curriculum are unmatched!

t_ch: 不管再怎么努力，我始终…不属于那个世界。
t_en: No matter how hard I work, I will never... belong to that world.

t_ch: 很多人都称赞我的努力。但他们不知道…那只是因为我一无所有。
t_en: Many people praise my hard work. But, what they don't know is... it is all because I have nothing.

t_ch: 我拼命学习，是为了折纸大学的奖学金，为了那份荣誉…为了找到一份好工作……
t_en: All of my hard work studying is so I can attain the Paperfold University Scholarship. For that honor... And for finding a good job...

t_ch: 只有这样，我才能留在这里，从无数学生中脱颖而出，有机会实现自己的梦想。
t_en: That is the only way that I will be able to stay here, stand out amongst the countless other students, and have a chance to achieve my dreams.

t_ch: 没什么问题。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 你已经做的很好了。
t_en: You have done extremely well.

t_ch: 但…这里是匹诺康尼，梦境里的人们大多身世显赫，对地位与价值更在乎，也更敏感。
t_en: But... this is Penacony. The majority of people from the Dreamscape are from notable backgrounds and are far more interested in and wary of status and value.

t_ch: 比如老是来烦我的那家伙，要不是因为惹到他，恐怕他绝对不会和我有任何交集。
t_en: It's like that guy who is always bothering me. If I hadn't angered him, I fear he never would have bothered interacting with me at all.

t_ch: 老实说，那家伙也有点可取之处。对那些从最开始就拥有许多东西的人来说，能为了证明自己而不懈努力，这也是个挺难能可贵的品质。
t_en: If I'm honest, there are things I could learn from that guy. For someone like him who has always been blessed with abundance from birth, that attitude of always working hard to prove oneself is actually a very precious quality.

t_ch: 所以偶尔我会想，我和他…是否有共同前行的可能？
t_en: So I sometimes think to myself... maybe he and I could find a common thread?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你别当真，只是无谓的思绪罢了。不管再怎么努力，我始终…不属于那个世界。
t_en: Hahaha, ignore me. They're just pointless thoughts... No matter how hard I work, I... I will never belong to that world.
